channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Meeting on shipping emissions could result in the world's first global carbon tax
item.description: Many of the world's largest shipping nations will decide Friday whether to impose a tax on carbon emissions from ships. If the 174 International Maritime Organization members present — all but the United States — reach an agreement, it would effectively be the world's first global carbon tax.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In fight over insurance, neighbors crowdsource LA fire contamination data
item.description: All sense of survivors’ guilt was fleeting for those residents whose homes remained standing after wildfires ripped through the Los Angeles area three months ago.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER and SALLY HO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Escaped California prison inmate convicted of murder kills police commander in Mexico: report
item.description: Cesar Hernandez, a prison inmate convicted of murder, allegedly was involved in a shootout with Mexican authorities months after escaping from California authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Suspected of backing a coup plot against the longtime dictator of Gambia nearly 20 years ago, Pharing Sanyang described Thursday how he was beaten with pipes and palm tree branches, pistol-whipped and hit in the face with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must facilitate return of deported Maryland man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration must facilitate the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GiveSendGo defends fundraiser for track meet stabbing suspect
item.description: Karmelo Anthony, 17, was arrested in the fatal stabbing of another Texas high school student, Austin Metcalf.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: A New York City sightseeing helicopter has broken apart in midair and crashed into the Hudson River, killing six people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: Hundreds of foreign students at dozens of colleges across the country have had their higher education experience turned upside down as the Trump administration has expanded its immigration crackdown beyond those involved in the pro-Palestinian protests.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Uber releases list of the strangest things left in its cars
item.description: It's no surprise that phones, wallets, and keys are the most common items left behind in Uber cars. But a urinal and a taxidermied rabbit? Uber has released its 2025 "Lost & Found Index," listing the 50 most unique items left behind in its rideshare vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 19 Alabama restaurants claiming imported shrimp is locally sourced: report
item.description: "Until more enforcement is in place, Gulf Shores area residents and visitors are urged to stay vigilant," the release said.
item.dc:creator: Summer Poole, NewsNation

item.title: Boozy battle: Maryland and Delaware fight over 'Orange Crush' cocktail
item.description: Lawmakers in Maryland voted Monday to approve the orange crush as the Old Line state's official state cocktail.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: UTA announces 'significant' changes to several bus services including new routes
item.description: The Utah Transit Authority said significant changes are coming to its services in Davis, Salt Lake, and Utah County.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: Supreme Court tells Trump administration to facilitate return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said President Donald Trump’s administration must facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jewish families channel Passover story after devastating losses in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: PASADENA, California (AP) — Aty Rotter lost her family home and her spiritual home in the ravenous fire that scorched a large swath of Los Angeles County earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah man arrested, charged for trying to rent out fake Airbnb in Hawaii
item.description: A man was arrested in Utah after allegedly charging thousands of dollars for a fake vacation rental in Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed during a merengue concert, killing at least 184 people and injuring over 200 more.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Texas lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration will screen for antisemitic activity in granting immigration benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's administration will immediately start considering “antisemitic activity” on social media and physical harassment of Jewish people as grounds for denying immigration benefits, according to a federal agency announcement on Wednesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Students react to nearly 50 international students' visas being revoked, records being removed across Utah
item.description: International students at institutions across Utah and around the country are having their records terminated or their visas revoked. Here's what some students said about it.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Exclusive: Pence agrees Trump’s tariff policy a potential midterm liability for GOP
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the Trump administration’s aggressive use of tariffs poses a potential risk for Republicans in the midterm elections, even as he expressed relief about a walk-back of some of the most severe tariffs on allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dolphins player Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute, police say
item.description: SUNNY ISLES BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Miami Dolphins wide receiver Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute at their South Florida high-rise condominium, but no charges were filed, according to a police report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VP of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: He was charged with promoting a sexual performance by a child and possessing a sexual performance by a child.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: State leaders tell Trump cabinet members that Utah should run its own federal programs
item.description: Utah's Speaker of the House Mike Schultz is among the top state leaders pushing for Utah to have control over federal programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, ABC 4

item.title: Tips for spring gardening: Utah experts talk best times to prep and plant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Gardening can be many things to different people—sometimes a chore, other times a passion. If you're one of those passionate gardeners or someone looking to get started, here are some tips from experts at Millcreek Gardens to help you get your garden started.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania judge is convicted of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend as he slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A magistrate judge in Pennsylvania accused of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend in the head as he slept was convicted Wednesday of attempted homicide and aggravated assault charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs worry US shoppers who buy groceries at Asian supermarkets
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Loyal customers of Asian supermarkets and other grocery stores that specialize in selling imported food heaved a collective sigh of dismay when President Donald Trump announced extra-high U.S. tariffs on goods from dozens of countries.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and KARENA PHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
